Hey, what's going on everyone? Okay, so the second half of that original setup is how to set up your Instagram profile. It follows more or less the same rules as the Facebook one. Who do you help do what without boo? It's nice to say like, hey, I help busy moms lose weight without giving up their kids or their lifestyle. I've helped over 800 moms to date lose over 2,000 pounds, whatever, and then you have two arrows beneath it and says, link to apply or schedule a call. We're downloading my free book on how we do this, blah, blah, blah, blah, all right, that is it. So who you help, some stat for credibility, and then link to apply and schedule, make sure that your actual profile picture on Instagram is a face picture, because it's so small that the only thing that you can really see is a face. You wanna make sure that that is your picture, and then make all of your pictures and posts about fitness. Okay, I can't say this again. If you're in a business, this is your business. So you need to look like a professional. It's the pictures you have should always relate back to fitness or eating healthy, because or client testimonials, because that is what is going to drive traffic for you people. The first thing you're gonna look at is your profile and make sure that you're a real looking normal person, and show that you actually are legit, okay? So the objective here is authority, social proof, and value first. That is what both of the objectives are for what we did with Facebook, and the same thing for Instagram, is we wanna show that we're in authority. We wanna show that we've done this before, and we wanna provide value before they even started working with us. That is the goal.
